# AI vs Pulled — Visual Lane

**Question answered:** How do users tell, at a glance, which content on a screen is computed from data vs generated by AI?

**Pattern ID:** `VIZ-PATTERN-AI-001`
**Binds rules:** workspace ADR-005 (AI-first thinking pattern), A11Y-008 (color is not the only encoding)

---

## When to use

Any analytics surface that mixes:

- **Pulled lane** — trends, averages, comparative metrics, distributions, leaderboards (computed from data)
- **AI lane** — themes, insights, action plans, summaries (LLM-extracted from open-text or generated)

PCE/CFE is the canonical case (Aarti audit ADR-005). The pattern generalizes: any time AI generates user-facing content, the affordance must distinguish it from pulled data so users can trust appropriately.

## The principle

Users must NEVER mistake AI-generated content for ground truth. Trust is asymmetric — over-trusting AI hides errors; under-trusting computed data wastes value.

## Visual treatments

### Pulled-lane content

| Element | Treatment |
|---|---|
| Charts (bullet, strip plot, heatmap, line, etc.) | Standard DS chart styling. No AI badge. |
| Metric numbers | Standard `tabular-nums` typography. No special chrome. |
| Tables | Standard DS `Table` / `DataTable`. |
| Last-updated timestamp | Refers to last data sync. |

### AI-lane content

| Element | Treatment |
|---|---|
| Container | DS `Banner` variant OR a distinct card with the AI affordance below |
| AI affordance (icon + label) | `fa-light fa-sparkles` (NOT `fa-star-christmas` — that's reserved for Leo). Icon color: `var(--brand-color)`. Label: "AI insight" |
| Content tone | Specific, action-oriented; cites source ("Based on…") |
| Edit affordance | Per ADR-005: accept / edit / clear / type-own. Visible when content is editable (action plans). |
| "Why" link | Optional in Phase 1; defer "explain why" to Phase 2 |

### The AI affordance — exact spec

```tsx
<div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground mb-2">
  <i
    className="fa-light fa-sparkles"
    style={{ color: 'var(--brand-color)' }}
    aria-hidden="true"
  />
  <span>AI insight</span>
</div>
```

Don't deviate from this on AI-lane surfaces. Consistency = trust.

## Anatomy

```
┌─ Course detail: PHARM 101 ────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                            │
│  ┌─ Pulled lane ────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Average rating: 4.2 / 5    Trend: ↗ +0.3            │ │
│  │  Response rate: 87%         vs cohort: +5pp          │ │
│  │  ▁▂▃▅▇▇▆▆ (sparkline last 6 terms)                  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                            │
│  ┌─ AI lane ─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ✨ AI insight                                         │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │  Students consistently mention pacing in the second   │ │
│  │  half. Comments cluster around lecture density        │ │
│  │  weeks 8–10. Consider redistributing content or        │ │
│  │  adding a checkpoint quiz week 9.                      │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │  Based on 47 open-text responses · 6 themes           │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │  [ Create action plan ]   [ Edit ]   [ Clear ]        │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## A11y notes

- AI affordance icon is `aria-hidden="true"` (decorative); label "AI insight" carries the meaning
- AI lane container has `role="region"` with `aria-label="AI insight"`
- Edit affordances are visible buttons (not icon-only) so screen readers announce them clearly
- Source citation ("Based on 47 responses") is plain text below the insight, NOT a tooltip (must be screen-reader accessible without hover)

## Tone for AI content

Aarti's directive (ADR-005):

> "AI is good at finding themes and grouping the information by themes. Just let AI do that work."

Bad AI content:
- ❌ "Average rating is 4.2." (that's pulled, not AI)
- ❌ "Things are going well." (vague, no theme)
- ❌ "Multiple students mentioned the course." (no theme extracted)

Good AI content:
- ✅ "Students consistently mention pacing in the second half. Comments cluster around weeks 8–10."
- ✅ "Three themes emerge: communication clarity, accessibility of office hours, and exam difficulty calibration."
- ✅ "One outlier comment flags safety concern in lab section 3 — review separately."

## Code recipe — admin profile

```tsx
'use client'
import { Banner, Button } from '@exxat/ds/packages/ui/src'

type AiInsightProps = {
  title: string
  body: string
  source: string  // e.g., "47 open-text responses · 6 themes"
  actions?: Array<{ label: string; onClick: () => void; variant?: 'default' | 'outline' }>
}

export function AiInsight({ title, body, source, actions = [] }: AiInsightProps) {
  return (
    <div role="region" aria-label="AI insight" className="rounded-lg border border-border p-4 bg-background">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground mb-2">
        <i
          className="fa-light fa-sparkles"
          style={{ color: 'var(--brand-color)' }}
          aria-hidden="true"
        />
        <span>{title}</span>
      </div>

      <p className="text-sm text-foreground mb-2">{body}</p>

      <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground mb-3">Based on {source}</p>

      {actions.length > 0 && (
        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
          {actions.map((action) => (
            <Button
              key={action.label}
              size="sm"
              variant={action.variant ?? 'outline'}
              onClick={action.onClick}
            >
              {action.label}
            </Button>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ AI content visually identical to pulled-data content — ADR-005 violation
- ❌ Mixing pulled metrics inside the AI lane card — confuses provenance
- ❌ Using `fa-star-christmas` for AI affordance — that's reserved for Leo specifically
- ❌ "Powered by AI" footer text without per-card affordance — too easy to miss
- ❌ AI content without source citation — users can't validate
- ❌ Auto-applying AI suggestions without explicit user accept — ADR-005 requires accept/edit/clear/type-own
- ❌ Editorialization in AI text ("This is concerning") — describe themes, don't judge
